headline: 26/40 maintainer-adjudicated regressions detected at the introducing PR
method: Run the shipped jrs:java-review-agent finders on the diff that INTRODUCED a bug, score recall against the defect the maintainers' later fix PR proves. Finders-only (upper-bound recall). 0 fabrications across all runs.

The async-ification in #25352 DELAYS the producer-creation callbacks. When a client-side createProducer times out, the client sends CloseProducer then a RETRY createProducer with the SAME producerId. The fix PR's own race table: 'The delaye

ConcurrentRoaringBitSet's read methods (get, nextSetBit, ...) use rwLock.tryOptimisticRead() and call super.<read>() on the RoaringBitmap with NO lock held, concurrently with a writer holding writeLock. This optimistic-read pattern is safe

The added AdaptiveByteBuf.setCharSequence override guards with checkIndex(index, sequence.length()) — a CHAR count — then delegates the write to rootParent().setCharSequence(idx(index), ...), where the byte-length bounds check runs against

#20990's added conditional runs removePendingAcks (whose unblock check reads the unacked count) BEFORE addAndGetUnAckedMsgs decrements it, and gates the decrement on its return; with maxUnackedMessagesPerConsumer=1 the consumer stays blocked forever. (Second adjudicated regression of the same diff, the sticky individualAck flag fixed by #21126, WAS found @HIGH by all 3 finders.)

#17007's cached(String) does TWO passes over the input: (1) new AsciiString(string) internally converts every char to a byte (a full scan), then (2) an added explicit for-loop scans the whole string again to detect any char > MAX_CHAR_VALUE

With allow_duplicates:false, appending a value that is already present (a no-op de-dup) still UPCASTS the existing scalar field into a single-element list, even though nothing changed. Correct behavior: leave the field as a scalar when the

#20158's parse paths call ledgerEntry.getEntryBuffer() (returns the pooled direct ByteBuf, transfers no ownership) but never release() it, unlike the prior getEntry() which copied+released. A pooled direct-memory ByteBuf leaks on every snap

Math.clamp(value, min, max) requires min <= max; here min=4096, max=n, so when the vector count n < 4096 it throws IllegalArgumentException. The old Math.min(n, Math.max(...)) returned n in that case (capping the sample at corpus size). Rea

getProjectedIds() omits list/map CONTAINER field IDs (only element/key/value IDs are added), whereas indexById() records every field ID. So allUsedFieldIds() under-reports used IDs; reassignConflictingIds walks candidate IDs from 1 and can

workerExec is reused as the RPC connectExec. ServiceClientImpl completes response/service-location futures on connectExec, while verifyAndMergeCheckpoints submits per-task-group work to workerExec and then blocks that worker thread in coale

HbckChore.java:226 -- relying on the in-memory RegionState alone. RegionState.isSplit() == (state == State.SPLIT); after a master restart/failover the in-memory assignment state resets (split parents load as CLOSED, not SPLIT), so isSplit()

RDBStore.close() calls flushDB() unconditionally, but the store can be opened READ-ONLY; flushing a read-only RocksDB handle is illegal and throws (caught+logged as WARN, so the observable effect is a spurious error on every read-only store

PendingDataNodeMessages.removeQueuedBlock() (line ~111-133) obtains its queue via getBlockQueue(block) (lines 120/122), and getBlockQueue lazily allocates + inserts an empty LinkedList into the persistent queueByBlockId for any absent block

Tasks.run() with a non-null service takes runParallel: it submits the single task and then blocks the caller in waitFor(futures), which busy-polls Thread.sleep(10) until completion. So report() still blocks the caller for the full HTTP POST

NonClosingReaderWrapper (FilterDirectoryReader) overrides only doClose() (no-op for the delegate) but not close(), so the base close() still sets closed=true. Caching + sharing one instance across searchers means that once one wrapped searc

The added `listener.onResponse(null)` completes the replica listener with SUCCESS on the closed-primary path. ReplicationOperation counts that as successful=1, failures=[], so the overall write is acknowledged to the client -- but the stale

notes: review_grade A/B = defect on the introducing diff's surface, C = cross-file, per evals/mined-pairs-candidates.md. 'deeper-than-fix' unadjudicated findings are deliberately NOT in these headline fields. Two mis-graded candidates (iceberg#14059, dubbo#4038) were dropped pre-scoring and are not counted.
